name: Re-entry Corridor — Atmospheric Interface
description: The narrow band of atmosphere where survival is possible — 6 degrees wide, 400,000 feet above the Pacific Ocean. The spacecraft is a falling stone; the corridor is a needle's eye.
atmosphericDetails: The first sign of atmosphere is a faint pink glow at the edges of the heat shield — plasma building as the ship decelerates from 25,000 miles per hour
The communications blackout of re-entry lasts 3 minutes and 14 seconds; in Houston, no one speaks
The g-forces build to 6.5 times normal gravity; the crew is pressed into their seats as though by a physical hand
Through the window, the plasma sheath turns from pink to orange to white — the color of the Sun, inches from the glass
Then the parachutes deploy, and the roar becomes a flutter, and the Pacific Ocean is below, grey and enormous and safe
